title: kanipakkam
description: Kanipakkam is a prominent pilgrimage center in the Chittoor district of Andhra Pradesh, India, famous for the Sri Varasidhi Vinayaka Swamy Temple. The temple is unique as the idol of Lord Ganesha is believed to be growing in size over time. According to legend, the idol was discovered in a well by three mute, deaf, and blind brothers, and the water from the well is considered holy. The temple architecture is magnificent, attracting thousands of devotees daily, especially during the Vinayaka Chaturthi festival.
travel_methods: The best way to reach Kanipakkam is via road. The nearest major railway station is Chittoor (11 km) or Tirupati (75 km). Tirupati Airport is the closest airport (approx. 80 km). State-run buses (APSRTC) and private taxis are readily available from Chittoor and Tirupati.
